project.name: opensre
project.description: Open-source SRE agent for automated incident investigation and root cause analysis. Connects 60+ observability/incident tools, runs LangGraph-driven agentic investigations, and ships scored synthetic RCA suites.

nodes.name: Alert Ingest & Normalization
nodes.summary: Receives heterogeneous alert payloads from Slack, Grafana, Datadog, PagerDuty, Vercel, OpsGenie and other sources, parses and normalizes them, then extracts the structured incident details (service, severity, time window) the rest of the agent reasons against.

businessRules: Every alert must be normalized to a canonical shape before downstream nodes run
LLM extraction must have a deterministic fallback when no LLM is configured
Sparse alerts (Datadog/Grafana) require full-JSON prompt instead of summary-only
crossDomainInteractions: Feeds the Investigation Pipeline domain with structured AlertDetails
Triggers the Hosted Runtime SSE/poll surfaces

nodes.name: Investigation Pipeline
nodes.summary: The agentic LangGraph state machine that drives an investigation: auth, integration resolution, alert extraction, time-window adaptation, planning, parallel hypothesis execution, root-cause diagnosis (with claim validation), and publishing. This is the heart of the agent.

businessRules: Every node is a typed transform on shared AgentState
Investigation loop terminates on iteration cap, sufficient evidence, or healthy verdict
Diagnosis short-circuits when system is clearly healthy or vendor evidence is missing
Claims must be validated against evidence before driving the verdict
crossDomainInteractions: Calls Tool Orchestration to execute action plans
Reads resolved configs from Integration Onboarding
Hands the final report to Report Publishing

nodes.name: Tool Orchestration
nodes.summary: Auto-discovers ~60+ vendor tool packages via pkgutil, exposes a typed registry by surface (investigation/chat), and executes actions in parallel per hypothesis with bounded retries on transient failures. Ties the planner's intent to concrete API calls against Datadog, Grafana, Kubernetes, AWS, GitHub, Sentry, Vercel, etc.

businessRules: Tools are auto-discovered at startup; no manual registration
Transient errors are retried; permanent errors propagate as captured exceptions
Each tool declares strict metadata (name/description/input_schema/source/surfaces)
Investigation surface and chat surface are independently filtered
crossDomainInteractions: Reuses vendor API clients from Integration Onboarding catalog

nodes.name: Integration Onboarding & Config
nodes.summary: Captures, validates, persists, and resolves integration credentials for 30+ vendors. Drives the interactive wizard, the JSON-backed integrations store with v1->v2 migration, the env-var catalog parser, and per-service health probes used by the verify command.

businessRules: Local store takes precedence over env vars during merge
Credentials must validate against live APIs before being persisted
Each vendor has a strict Pydantic config model and a probe function
Schema migrations from v1 to v2 happen lazily on load
crossDomainInteractions: Provides resolved integration configs to the Investigation Pipeline (resolve_integrations node)
Drives the Hosted Runtime's remote integration resolution

nodes.name: Report Publishing & Delivery
nodes.summary: Builds the final RCA report with evidence catalog and source provenance, applies guardrail/masking redaction, then fans out delivery to Slack, Discord, Telegram, GitLab MR notes, terminal renderer, editor, and ingest endpoints. Keeps side-effecting transports isolated from upstream graph nodes.

businessRules: Sensitive data must be masked before delivery
Each transport handles its own auth and error redaction
GitLab writeback truncates oversized bodies
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes the verdict and evidence from Investigation Pipeline
Reuses guardrails to block/redact unsafe content

nodes.name: Hosted Runtime & Deployment
nodes.summary: Operates OpenSRE as a hosted service: a FastAPI server with /investigate (sync + SSE streaming) and deep-health endpoints, the remote agent client, Vercel poller for incoming failures, Clerk JWT auth, and the deployment drivers (Railway, LangSmith/LangGraph Platform, EC2). Includes an MCP server entrypoint for IDE/CLI clients.

businessRules: All hosted endpoints require a valid Clerk JWT (Bearer token)
Deployments must pass health polling before being marked successful
Provider configs are dry-run validated before any infra is touched
JWKS keys are cached per-issuer with TTL and async refresh
crossDomainInteractions: Drives the Investigation Pipeline remotely via the same LangGraph entry
Fetches integrations through Integration Onboarding's resolution layer
Streams events the CLI renders identically to local runs

nodes.name: Normalize Incoming Alert
nodes.summary: Converts a raw vendor-specific alert payload (Datadog/Grafana/Slack/Vercel/etc.) into the canonical AlertPayload shape used everywhere downstream.

nodes.name: Parse CLI args & dispatch
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI driver chooses between alert-template generation and full investigation execution based on parsed argv.

nodes.name: Build or load alert payload
nodes.summary: Reads alert payload from disk, stdin, or constructs one from CLI flags before handing it to the runner.

nodes.name: Normalize alert payload
nodes.summary: Canonical alert-payload normalizer that converts source-specific (Datadog/Grafana/Vercel/etc.) alerts into a unified shape.

nodes.name: Seed initial AgentState
nodes.summary: Constructs an AgentState for an incoming alert by normalizing payload, extracting OpenRCA scoring points, and setting investigation start time.

nodes.name: Extract Alert Details with LLM
nodes.summary: Runs the structured-output LLM on the raw alert payload to produce typed AlertDetails (service, severity, time range), enriches the raw alert, and pushes a Slack acknowledgement.

nodes.name: Decide summary vs full-JSON prompt
nodes.summary: Heuristic that returns True when the alert source produces sparse summaries (Datadog/Grafana) so the LLM is given the full raw JSON instead of summary-only.

nodes.name: Invoke structured-output LLM
nodes.summary: Runs the structured-output LLM on the raw alert payload to produce AlertDetails, choosing between summary-only and full-JSON prompts depending on source.

nodes.name: Fallback to deterministic extractor
nodes.summary: Builds a best-effort AlertDetails directly from the raw alert dict when LLM extraction is unavailable or fails, ensuring the graph always has structured details.

nodes.name: Enrich raw alert with extracted fields
nodes.summary: Merges fields from extracted AlertDetails back into the raw alert dict so downstream nodes see a unified payload regardless of source.

nodes.name: Build problem markdown & push Slack ack
nodes.summary: Renders a short markdown problem statement and emits a Slack acknowledgement reaction so the user sees the agent picked up the alert.

nodes.name: Adapt Incident Time Window
nodes.summary: Inspects the previous iteration's executed actions and, when deploy-timeline came back empty, returns a strictly-wider incident window so subsequent queries hit data.

nodes.name: Read last-iteration action outcomes
nodes.summary: Pulls the executed-actions dict from the most recent investigation iteration, defending against shape drift in upstream state.

nodes.name: Widen window when timeline empty
nodes.summary: Inspects the previous iteration's executed actions and, when deploy-timeline came back empty, returns a strictly-wider incident window plus rationale.

nodes.name: Emit time-window state delta
nodes.summary: Invokes adapt_incident_window on the current state and converts the rule's plain-dict output into a LangGraph state delta, logging when the window changed.

nodes.name: Compile & Run LangGraph Pipeline
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical LangGraph state machine wiring auth, chat, alert extraction, parallel investigation, merge, evaluation, and publishing nodes, then runs it sync or streaming against an alert payload.

nodes.name: Inject auth context
nodes.summary: First-run LangGraph node — extracts auth context (org_id/user_id/email/thread_id/run_id) from the RunnableConfig and merges it into AgentState.

nodes.name: Resolve integrations from local + remote
nodes.summary: Resolves integrations from local config and/or remote tracer service, decoding org id from the JWT and tracking errors per source.

nodes.name: Build graph topology
nodes.summary: Constructs the LangGraph state machine by wiring auth, chat, alert extraction, parallel investigation, merge, evaluation, and routing edges into a compiled runnable.

nodes.name: Compile graph & invoke
nodes.summary: Synchronously runs an investigation by compiling the graph and invoking it with an alert payload and resolved integrations.

nodes.name: Stream LangGraph events
nodes.summary: Async generator that streams LangGraph events for an investigation, yielding mapped StreamEvent objects to the caller.

nodes.name: Route chat vs investigation mode
nodes.summary: Top-level mode router for the unified chat/investigation graph; returns the next-node label based on AgentState mode.

nodes.name: Plan Investigation Actions
nodes.summary: Selects the next batch of tool calls by detecting available sources, extracting keywords, seeding mandatory actions, and invoking the planner LLM with a richly assembled prompt — all bounded by a tool budget.

nodes.name: Detect available investigation sources
nodes.summary: Walks a raw alert payload, alert context, and resolved integrations to determine every investigation source available, returning a normalized list.

nodes.name: Extract query keywords
nodes.summary: Tokenizes problem markdown and alert name, filters stop words, and returns ordered unique keywords for query construction.

nodes.name: Seed mandatory must-run actions
nodes.summary: Guarantees that mandatory seed actions for the detected sources are present in the action pool within tool budget constraints.

nodes.name: Build planner prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the full investigation planning prompt combining problem markdown, executed hypotheses, available actions, sources, and memory context.

nodes.name: Invoke planner LLM
nodes.summary: Invokes the planner LLM with the assembled prompt and parses its structured response into a typed plan model.

nodes.name: Filter, rank & apply tool budget
nodes.summary: Filters and ranks candidate actions against available sources, executed hypotheses, and tool budget to produce the final ordered action list.

nodes.name: Emit plan & detect reroute
nodes.summary: LangGraph node entry that orchestrates planning: gathers state, calls plan_actions, masks sensitive fields, and returns the state delta with reroute detection.

nodes.name: Execute Hypotheses in Parallel
nodes.summary: Fans planned hypotheses out to per-branch workers that resolve actions against the tool registry, run them with bounded retries, and emit per-hypothesis ActionExecutionResults.

nodes.name: Distribute hypotheses to branches
nodes.summary: Fans hypotheses out to parallel investigation branches in the LangGraph pipeline.

nodes.name: Worker resolves available actions
nodes.summary: Per-hypothesis worker: tracks timing, resolves available investigation actions, runs them, and returns the per-hypothesis result for the merge step.

nodes.name: Execute action with retry
nodes.summary: Executes a single registered action with bounded retries on transient errors, capturing parameters, runtime, output, and any final exception.

nodes.name: Classify transient errors
nodes.summary: Classifies an exception as transient (retryable) by matching its message against a curated list of network/timeout/rate-limit signatures.

nodes.name: Merge hypothesis results
nodes.summary: Combines per-hypothesis execution outputs, masking-aware text, and OpenSRE seed telemetry into the investigation state, post-processing through evidence summarization.

nodes.name: Summarize execution results
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestration entry that merges new execution results, builds the evidence summary, tracks hypotheses, and returns the consolidated state delta.

nodes.name: Diagnose Root Cause
nodes.summary: Drives the final LLM judgment with claim validation, vendor-evidence checks, and short-circuits for healthy systems or insufficient evidence.

nodes.name: Check evidence availability
nodes.summary: Inspects the investigation context, collected evidence, and raw alert to determine whether enough vendor evidence is present to run an LLM diagnosis.

nodes.name: Short-circuit on clearly healthy
nodes.summary: Heuristic detector that returns True when the alert and evidence indicate a clearly healthy system, allowing the diagnosis loop to short-circuit.

nodes.name: Build per-vendor diagnosis prompt
nodes.summary: Top-level prompt assembler that combines memory context, vendor directives, and per-source evidence sections into the diagnosis LLM prompt.

nodes.name: Invoke reasoning LLM
nodes.summary: Core LangGraph node action: builds the diagnosis prompt, calls the reasoning LLM, parses claims, and dispatches to healthy-finding or insufficient-evidence terminals.

nodes.name: Validate claims against evidence
nodes.summary: Validates a single diagnosis claim against the collected evidence dict, checking vendor-specific signals to assert support level.

nodes.name: Compute validity score
nodes.summary: Computes an overall validity score from validated and non-validated claim sets to drive confidence reporting.

nodes.name: Decide investigation loop continuation
nodes.summary: Loop controller for the planner→action→diagnose cycle; decides continue/exit based on state.investigation_loop_count and verdict.

nodes.name: Auto-Discover Tool Registry
nodes.summary: Walks the app.tools package via pkgutil at startup, harvests every BaseTool subclass and @tool-decorated function, and exposes cached lookups by surface (investigation/chat).

nodes.name: Walk tool modules via pkgutil
nodes.summary: Iterates discoverable tool modules and imports each so their RegisteredTool entries surface.

nodes.name: Collect RegisteredTools from module
nodes.summary: Inspects an imported tool module for BaseTool subclasses or decorated functions and yields their RegisteredTool representations.

nodes.name: Apply @tool decorator metadata
nodes.summary: Implementation of the @tool decorator that detects whether to attach RegisteredTool metadata to a BaseTool or wrap a plain function.

nodes.name: Cache registry snapshot
nodes.summary: Iterates discoverable tool modules, imports each, and assembles the deduplicated tuple of RegisteredTool entries that backs the registry cache.

nodes.name: Filter by surface
nodes.summary: Public API returning all RegisteredTool entries filtered to a given surface (investigation or chat).

nodes.name: Execute Action Against Vendor
nodes.summary: Looks up a planned action in the registry, invokes the vendor tool with bounded retries, and captures the structured result for the evidence catalog.

nodes.name: Lookup registered tool
nodes.summary: Looks up each requested action in the registry by name to obtain its RegisteredTool entry.

nodes.name: Invoke with bounded retry
nodes.summary: Executes a single registered action with bounded retries on transient errors, capturing parameters, runtime, output, and any final exception.

nodes.name: Capture ActionExecutionResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the outcome of executing a single registered action: name, params, output, duration, and any exception raised.

nodes.name: Driver collects all results
nodes.summary: Top-level driver: looks up each requested action in the registry, runs it through _execute_with_retry, and returns the collected results.

nodes.name: Invoke LLM Provider
nodes.summary: Provider-agnostic LLM client layer wrapping Anthropic, Bedrock, OpenAI, Gemini, and CLI providers with structured output, tool binding, and retry semantics.

nodes.name: Normalize messages per provider
nodes.summary: Normalizes prompt strings or message lists into the provider-specific chat format (Anthropic, OpenAI).

nodes.name: Dispatch by provider
nodes.summary: Picks the right primary chat model and tool model for the configured provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), raising UnsupportedChatProviderError when unknown.

nodes.name: Invoke with retry & timeout
nodes.summary: Sends the request via the provider SDK with retry semantics and timeout handling.

nodes.name: Parse structured output
nodes.summary: Extracts the first JSON object from a free-form LLM text response and validates against the requested schema.

nodes.name: Run Onboarding Wizard
nodes.summary: When a new user runs `opensre`, the questionary-driven wizard walks them through LLM provider selection, integration selection, credential capture, live validation, and persistence.

nodes.name: Greet user & pick LLM provider
nodes.summary: Top-level wizard entry point: greets the user, orchestrates LLM provider selection, integration configuration, and writes the resulting config.

nodes.name: Select integrations to configure
nodes.summary: Iterates the user-selected integrations, dispatches each to its dedicated _configure_* handler, and aggregates per-integration results.

nodes.name: Capture per-vendor credentials
nodes.summary: Captures vendor-specific credentials (AWS keys, GitHub MCP, Splunk endpoint, OpenClaw URL/auth, etc.) interactively with secret masking.

nodes.name: Validate credentials against live APIs
nodes.summary: Probes each configured integration against its real remote API and surfaces typed validation errors before persisting.

nodes.name: Bootstrap local Grafana/Loki demo (optional)
nodes.summary: Bootstraps a local Grafana+Loki stack via the bundled docker-compose definition and seeds it with sample logs for first-run demos.

nodes.name: Persist to integrations store
nodes.summary: Upserts a service-level record (wrapping into instances if needed) and persists the JSON-backed integrations store atomically.

nodes.name: Resolve Effective Integrations
nodes.summary: Merges the local store with environment-variable-sourced configs, classifies them by service across ~30 vendors, and produces the EffectiveIntegrations object the runtime consumes.

nodes.name: Load from JSON store
nodes.summary: Loads the integrations JSON file from disk and applies any pending v1->v2 migration before returning the dict.

nodes.name: Load from environment vars
nodes.summary: Reads OS environment variables for every supported integration vendor and constructs the env-sourced integration dict consumed by the catalog.

nodes.name: Merge store + env (store wins)
nodes.summary: Merges local store integrations with env-sourced integrations, with the local store taking precedence per service.

nodes.name: Classify by service & instance
nodes.summary: Massive per-service classifier that normalizes credentials and config for nearly every supported integration into a uniform classified record.

nodes.name: Publish EffectiveIntegrations
nodes.summary: Produces the EffectiveIntegrations object the runtime consumes by classifying, merging, and publishing per-service entries with provenance metadata.

nodes.name: Verify Integrations Health
nodes.summary: Runs a per-service ProbeResult against every configured integration's live API and produces a CLI-friendly status report plus a process exit code suitable for `make verify-integrations`.

nodes.name: Resolve effective integrations
nodes.summary: Returns the resolved EffectiveIntegrations view used by verify_integrations.

nodes.name: Dispatch per-service probes
nodes.summary: Runs the verifier for each configured service (or a specific service) and returns a list of structured per-service results.

nodes.name: Collect ProbeResults
nodes.summary: Each integration's verifier returns a ProbeResult (passed/failed/missing) with status, detail, and metadata.

nodes.name: Format human-readable report
nodes.summary: Renders a list of verification results as a human-readable status report for the CLI.

nodes.name: Compute process exit code
nodes.summary: Computes a process exit code from verification results, optionally narrowed to a single requested service.

nodes.name: Build & Deliver RCA Report
nodes.summary: Terminal node that builds the report context, masks secrets, formats the report, and fans out to all configured delivery channels (Slack, Discord, Telegram, terminal, GitLab, ingest, editor).

nodes.name: Build report context
nodes.summary: Top-level builder that normalizes state, extracts CloudWatch info, builds the source provenance catalog, attaches evidence to claims.

nodes.name: Attach evidence citations to claims
nodes.summary: Cross-references claims with the evidence catalog and source-to-id map, attaching display-friendly citation tags.

nodes.name: Apply masking & guardrails
nodes.summary: Runs the report through masking and guardrails to redact secrets and policy-violating content before delivery.

nodes.name: Send to Slack
nodes.summary: Top-level Slack report sender that picks the right transport (direct, webapp, or incoming webhook) based on configured credentials.

nodes.name: Send to Discord
nodes.summary: High-level helper that composes a Discord embed payload from an RCA report and sends it via post_discord_message.

nodes.name: Send to Telegram
nodes.summary: High-level helper that formats an RCA report as Telegram-flavored text and sends it via post_telegram_message.

nodes.name: Post GitLab MR writeback
nodes.summary: Posts the finished report as an MR note on GitLab, truncating overly long bodies and gracefully handling missing GitLab context.

nodes.name: Emit final state delta
nodes.summary: LangGraph node entry that wraps generate_report and returns the resulting state delta.

nodes.name: Apply Guardrails to Text
nodes.summary: Scans text against compiled rules, merges overlapping match spans, applies redact/block/audit actions, and writes audit entries — used both inbound and outbound.

nodes.name: Scan text against rules
nodes.summary: Scans text against configured rules, merges overlapping spans, redacts or blocks based on actions.

nodes.name: Merge overlapping match spans
nodes.summary: Merges overlapping rule match spans so the same characters aren't double-redacted.

nodes.name: Apply redact/block/audit action
nodes.summary: Applies the configured action for each rule (redact text, block the message, or only audit).

nodes.name: Write audit entry
nodes.summary: Writes audit entries via the injected audit sink so policy decisions are traceable.

nodes.name: Serve Investigation via FastAPI
nodes.summary: FastAPI server exposing /investigate (sync), /investigate/stream (SSE), deep-health checks, and Discord interaction handling — backed by the same LangGraph pipeline as the CLI.

nodes.name: Authenticate Clerk JWT
nodes.summary: Top-level @auth.authenticate handler that strips the Bearer prefix, verifies the Clerk JWT, and returns a MinimalUserDict with org identity.

nodes.name: Verify JWT against cached JWKS
nodes.summary: Asynchronously verifies a JWT against Clerk's JWKS: extracts unverified claims, picks the right issuer, fetches the signing key, and decodes the token.

nodes.name: Invoke /investigate endpoint
nodes.summary: Synchronous /investigate endpoint that runs an investigation and returns the final result and metadata.

nodes.name: Stream SSE events
nodes.summary: Streaming /investigate endpoint that emits Server-Sent Events as the LangGraph pipeline progresses through the investigation.

nodes.name: Parse SSE on client
nodes.summary: Parses a Server-Sent Events HTTP response into a stream of StreamEvent objects with extracted node names and details.

nodes.name: Deep health check endpoint
nodes.summary: Endpoint returning a deep health report covering LLM connectivity, disk, and memory checks.

nodes.name: Liveness /health endpoint
nodes.summary: FastAPI route handler for GET /health returning HealthResponse and setting status 200 or 503 based on overall readiness.

nodes.name: Poll Vercel for Failures
nodes.summary: Background poller that scans Vercel projects for failed deployments and runtime errors, builds canonical investigation candidates, and dispatches them through the agent.

nodes.name: Fetch deployment bundle
nodes.summary: Fetches a deployment along with its events and runtime logs from Vercel, optionally in parallel.

nodes.name: Parse Vercel URL
nodes.summary: Parses a Vercel dashboard URL into project, deployment, and selected-log identifiers usable by the poller.

nodes.name: Build canonical alert
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical alert representation for a Vercel deployment failure by combining project, deployment, events, and runtime logs.

nodes.name: Dedupe via candidate signature
nodes.summary: Constructs a VercelInvestigationCandidate with deduplication signature from a deployment + events + logs bundle.

nodes.name: Collect new candidates
nodes.summary: Polls Vercel projects for failed deployments and returns a list of investigation candidates not yet processed by the state store.

nodes.name: Enrich remote alert from Vercel
nodes.summary: Enriches an inbound alert payload with Vercel deployment, events, and runtime logs when a Vercel URL is referenced.

nodes.name: Deploy to Railway
nodes.summary: Railway CLI deployment driver that detects the CLI, validates auth, runs `railway up`, parses the deployment URL from stdout, polls health, and returns a typed result.

nodes.name: Detect Railway CLI on PATH
nodes.summary: Detect whether the Railway CLI is on PATH; precondition for the railway deploy method.

nodes.name: Validate Railway config
nodes.summary: Validates Railway deploy configuration ensuring api_token, project_id, and service_id are all populated.

nodes.name: Check Railway auth status
nodes.summary: Probes `railway whoami` to determine whether the local CLI is logged in and returns an auth-detail dict.

nodes.name: Run `railway up`
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full Railway deployment flow: validates auth, sets env vars, invokes `railway up`, polls health, and returns a DeployResult.

nodes.name: Extract deployment URL
nodes.summary: Extracts the deployment URL from Railway CLI stdout using best-effort regex matching of the up-command output.

nodes.name: Poll /health then /ok
nodes.summary: Repeatedly probes /health and /ok endpoints until one returns HTTP 200 or the attempt cap is exhausted.

nodes.name: Deploy to LangSmith
nodes.summary: Deploys the LangGraph hosted runtime to LangSmith Platform: resolves API key, validates against LangSmith, persists to .env, then invokes the LangGraph CLI deploy command.

nodes.name: Detect LangGraph CLI
nodes.summary: Detect whether the LangGraph CLI is on PATH; precondition for the langsmith deploy method.

nodes.name: Resolve LangSmith API key
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective LangSmith API key with the precedence CLI flag > .env > stored wizard credentials > environment variable.

nodes.name: Validate API key against LangSmith
nodes.summary: Validates a LangSmith API key by issuing an authenticated HTTP probe via httpx and surfacing typed error states.

nodes.name: Persist resolved env
nodes.summary: Persists the resolved LangSmith API key and deployment name into the project .env file without disturbing other entries.

nodes.name: Invoke LangGraph CLI deploy
nodes.summary: Invokes the LangGraph CLI deploy command against the LangSmith API and returns the captured CLI output.

nodes.name: Expose MCP Server Entrypoint
nodes.summary: FastMCP server exposing the OpenSRE investigation pipeline as a `run_rca` tool callable from MCP-compatible clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.).

nodes.name: Define run_rca input schema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model defining the run_rca tool's input schema: alert payload plus optional name, pipeline, and severity overrides.

nodes.name: Validate input & apply overrides
nodes.summary: MCP-registered tool that validates input, applies label/severity overrides, runs the investigation CLI, and returns a structured result.

nodes.name: Invoke investigation CLI
nodes.summary: Bridges the MCP request to run_investigation_cli and shapes the response into RunRCAOutput.

nodes.name: Launch FastMCP stdio server
nodes.summary: MCP server entrypoint — invokes mcp.run() to launch the FastMCP stdio server exposing the run_rca tool.

edges.description: AlertDetails and normalized AgentState flow into the LangGraph pipeline as the seed for planning, execution, and diagnosis.
EffectiveIntegrations are resolved per-run by the resolve_integrations node and dictate which sources/tools the planner can use.
Integration credentials hydrate vendor service clients (Datadog, Grafana, AlertManager, etc.) that tools call at execution time.
plan_actions and execute_actions look up RegisteredTools by name and run them with bounded retries inside parallel hypothesis branches.
publish_findings receives the verdict, evidence catalog, and validated claims from the diagnosis node and renders/dispatches the final report.
FastAPI /investigate endpoints and MCP run_rca call straight into the same compiled LangGraph runner used by the CLI.
Vercel poller and Slack-context handlers ingest external signals and synthesize alert payloads for the normalizer.
Remote runtime decodes the org_id from the Clerk JWT to fetch tenant-scoped integrations from the tracer service.
Slack/Discord/GitLab delivery transports reuse the same vendor service clients registered via the tool/integration layers.
